passages.title: A Rough Day at the Lake
passages.text:
Last summer, my family decided to spend a day at the lake. The sun was shining brightly, and the water looked beautiful. When I stepped into the water, it was as high as my chest. The ground beneath was rough, and I could feel small rocks under my feet.

My younger sister was pale with fear because she couldn't swim well. She screamed when a fish touched her leg. I tried not to disturb her more, so I quietly moved to the shade of a big tree nearby.

My father estimated that we had been there for about three hours. He said the dark clouds were a threat of a coming storm. As a consequence, we decided to leave early. Despite the short trip, it was a satisfying day that gave us strength for the week ahead.

questions.question: How deep was the water when the narrator stepped in?
questions.questionKr: 화자가 물에 들어갔을 때 물의 깊이는?

questions.question: Why did the family leave the lake early?
Where did the narrator go to rest?
acceptableAnswers: the shade of a big tree
questions.sampleAnswer: The narrator went to the shade of a big tree.

passages.title: The School Talent Show
passages.text:
Every year, our school holds a talent show to honor students with special skills. This year, I decided to enter the competition even though I had narrow experience in performing on stage.

At first, I was a victim of my own fear. My face turned pale, and I felt terror rising inside me. But my best friend gave me confidence by saying, "You can do it!" Her words impressed me deeply.

When I finally performed my dance, the audience loved it. The experience was a disaster in my imagination, but a success in reality. I learned that facing our fears builds inner strength. The show was a supplement to our regular school life, and it satisfied everyone who watched.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the school talent show?
How did the narrator feel before performing?
Who gave the narrator confidence?
questions.sampleAnswer: The narrator's best friend gave her confidence.
